text.title: Playwright Automation Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Test file uses .btn-primary, #form-submit, .css-1a2b3c — selectors that look stable but die on next CSS refactor.
antipattern.root-cause: Codegen generates CSS as a lowest-common-denominator fallback. The author shipped without converting to role/text locators.
antipattern.fix: Rewrite every CSS selector to getByRole/getByLabel/getByText. Only keep CSS when no semantic role exists, and add a comment explaining why.

antipattern.symptom: Suite has page.waitForTimeout(2000) sprinkled 'to be safe' after every action.
antipattern.root-cause: LLM defaulted to fixed sleeps to make tests pass locally; never tested under load.
antipattern.fix: Ban via CI lint rule (grep -F 'waitForTimeout'). Replace each occurrence with expect(locator).toBeVisible() or page.waitForLoadState().

antipattern.symptom: Agent saw 'strict mode violation' and changed getByRole('button') to page.locator('button').first().
antipattern.root-cause: Agent treated the error as a selector-too-narrow problem instead of selector-too-ambiguous.
antipattern.fix: Always narrow with { name: 'exact label' }. .first() silently tests an arbitrary element.

antipattern.symptom: auth.json appears in git history; pre-commit hooks did not catch it.
antipattern.root-cause: Author forgot to add auth.json to .gitignore; secret-scanning hooks not configured.
antipattern.fix: Add auth.json + storageState.json + .pwauth/ to .gitignore. Add a pre-commit hook that fails if any auth-shaped file is staged.

antipattern.symptom: CI artifact bucket fills weekly; 5-minute runs produce 200MB traces.
antipattern.root-cause: trace: 'on' captured every test, every action — most are never inspected.
antipattern.fix: Switch to trace: 'on-first-retry' (CI) or 'retain-on-failure'. Set screenshot: 'only-on-failure'.
